claim: Every applied proposal shall leave a trace entry in the runtime land-log (.runtime/land-log.jsonl).
why: Not yet ported in the Go port. The historical mechanism wrote spec/.runtime/land-log.jsonl -- the mechanical record of what tier (T1/T2) verified each applied proposal and whether pytest passed -- which gate_status.py read to answer the commit-boundary question (R-commit-boundary-checkable, hotam-spec-self); without it there was no auditable trail of what actually got verified before landing. `hotam land` (cmd/hotam/land.go) runs apply-proposal -> gen-spec -> all-violations but writes no runtime land-log, and gate_status is Declared (not ported) in methodology.Tools; no .runtime directory exists in the repo. The original WHY stands: without an auditable land-log trace there is no durable record of what actually got verified before landing. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists in the Go port.
